member.name: Chris Bloore
member.synopsis: Chris Bloore is the Labour MP for Redditch , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Cost of Living Payments: Redditch
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, how many households in Redditch are in receipt of cost of living support payments.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There have been no further Cost-of-Living payments since those issued in the financial year 2023/24. The Cost-of-Living Payments were intended to relieve the immediate financial pressures caused by the rapid rise in cost of living. There are no plans to r...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Poverty: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment he has made of levels of child poverty linked to the cost of living crisis in Redditch.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is working to address cost-of-living pressures facing families across the country through targeted measures including raising the minimum wage for workers aged 21 and over, which increased by 4.1 per cent to £12.71 an hour. The removal of t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Food Poverty: Redditch
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment he has made of food insecurity levels among households in Redditch.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data on the number of households experiencing food insecurity by constituency is not availa-ble due to sample sizes. Statistics on the number of individuals living in households that are food insecure by region in the UK are published annually in the “Fam...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Private Rented Housing: Redditch
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the affordability of private rented housing in Redditch.
writtenQuestions.answerText: According to the latest Office for National Statistics data, private renters in Redditch spent on average 29.1% of their income on rent. This compares to 29.2% in the West Midlands and 36.3% for England overall.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Debts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of trends in household energy debt in Redditch over the last two years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not made such an assessment and does not hold this specific data. The independent industry regulator Ofgem monitors and reports on levels of energy debt in Great Britain, and their latest statistical release can be found here: Debt and ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Credit: Redditch
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what estimate she has made of the number of people in Redditch using credit to pay for essential household bills.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises that, where household finances are stretched, some people may use credit to cover essential costs. That is why the Government has taken action to ease pressure on household finances, including by taking £150 off energy bills this...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Inflation: Redditch
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, what recent assessment she has made of inflationary pressures on household finances in Redditch.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Higher inflation in recent years has added to pressures on household finances, especially through the price of everyday essentials such as food and energy.The Office for National Statistics does not produce inflationary data at the constituency level. At ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Cost of Living: Redditch
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the cost of living crisis on low-income households in Redditch.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is working to address cost-of-living pressures facing families across the country through targeted measures including raising the minimum wage for workers aged 21 and over, which increased by 4.1 per cent to £12.71 an hour. The removal of t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Mental Health: Redditch
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the impact of rising living costs on mental health outcomes in Redditch.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We recognise the effect that financial pressures, such as those caused by rising living costs, can have on some people’s mental health, including in Redditch. The cross-Government, cross-sector suicide prevention strategy for England includes financial di...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Fraud
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what steps his Department is taking through the Integrated Risk and Intelligence Service to identify AI-generated employment scams.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department takes the risk posed by increasingly sophisticated scams enabled by artificial intelligence very seriously. DWP works closely with partners such as Action Fraud to prevent fraudulent activity online and suspected fraud and scams can also be...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Financial Services and Innovation: Vocational Education
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps Skills England is taking to integrate (a) applied financial literacy and (b) technical innovation modules into Level 3 technical qualifications.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Curriculum and Assessment Review recommended ways to ensure financial literacy is incorporated into the curriculum throughout all key stages of education, including post-16 education. There are a range of financial education qualifications available f...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Consultants
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what recent assessment she has made of the potential impact of AI automation on government consultancy spending.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government’s assessment has focused on how artificial intelligence can improve civil service roles, productivity and service delivery, including by automating routine tasks and supporting use of departmental capability. This work is being taken forwar...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Disinformation: Cryptography
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what steps the Government Communication Service is taking to implement cryptographic authentication and digital watermarking on official media to prevent the dissemination of deepfake government content.
writtenQuestions.answerText: GCS works closely with media partners and makes use of advanced technical capabilities, including artificial intelligence-driven tools, verification systems, and third-party brand safety tools, to ensure official content is served in safe, authentic, and ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Internet
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the effectiveness of the Online Safety Act 2023 in requiring platforms to detect and label AI-generated content.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Online Safety Act takes a proportionate approach to this issue by requiring in-scope services to tackle the most harmful content that is illegal or harmful to children, including where it is AI-generated.The Government will conduct a full statutory po...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Business: Investment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps his Department is taking to improve access to scale-up finance for employee-owned businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are improving access to scale-up finance for UK businesses, including the growing number of employee-owned businesses, through the British Business Bank (BBB). In November, we agreed the BBB’s five-year plan, which sets out how it will focus on scale-u...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Sovereign AI Fund
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the impact of the Sovereign AI Fund on reducing UK reliance on overseas AI infrastructure.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Sovereign AI Fund is designed to strengthen the UK’s capability in strategically important parts of the AI value chain, including compute and infrastructure. Backed by around £500 million, it combines equity investment, access to public compute, R&amp...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Energy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what recent discussions she has had with the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero on aligning AI-driven electricity demand forecasts with available grid capacity.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology engages regularly with the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero to ensure the UK energy system supports growing digital and AI‑driven demand. The Government recognises that rapid...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Ministers: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps she is taking through the Data and AI Ethics Framework to ensure the transparency and accuracy of generative AI in the drafting of ministerial advice.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Data and AI Ethics Framework provides detailed guidance and a self-assessment for government and public sector teams on how to use data and data-driven technologies responsibly. It bridges the gap between high-level ethical principles and practical ac...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Small Businesses: Cybersecurity

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Impact Assessment of the Cyber Security and Resilience (Network and Information Systems) Bill considers the impact on small and medium-sized enterprises. This is available on gov.uk. The Bill is also explicit that small and micro-sized managed or digi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Cybersecurity: Disclosure of Information
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what estimate she has made of the resources required for essential service providers to comply with the mandatory incident reporting requirements of the Cyber Security and Resilience Bi
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Impact Assessment of the Cyber Security and Resilience (Network and Information Systems) Bill provides estimates of the additional costs for businesses that will be impacted by the Bill and is available on GOV.UK.The government is developing further d...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
